behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the rule file, the example string '$IMURAW\0'.encode('ascii'), and mentions \N{...} named escapes which the tests check.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the problem (panic, false positive) without specifying the fix (from_str_radix, saturating_sub).

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for absence of panic and absence of UP012 triggering, not specific implementation details; alternative correct fixes would pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute the ruff binary via subprocess and inspect output/exit codes.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub wouldn't work since ruff must still build and not panic; the UP012 false-positive test requires actual detection logic.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover panic prevention, false-positive avoidance on \N{...}, and octal edge cases as described in instruction.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped under clear section headers (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, Config-derived).

structured_data_schema.reason: Task does not produce structured data.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction points to the rule file but does not name the buggy line or specific function; localization within that file is still required.
